message:
feat: implement core simulation structures with SoA layout and generational IDs

This commit establishes the foundational data structures and systems for the
ScriptBots artificial life simulation, implementing a modern, cache-friendly
architecture designed for high-performance parallel execution.

Core Data Structures (scriptbots-core):
- Migrated AgentId from simple u64 to slotmap-based generational handles,
  preventing stale references and enabling safe agent lifecycle management
- Implemented complete WorldState with arena-backed agent storage and FoodGrid
  using Structure-of-Arrays (SoA) friendly layout for optimal cache utilization
- Defined AgentState runtime structure with fixed-size sensor input buffers
  [f32; 25] and actuator output buffers [f32; 9] to eliminate per-tick heap
  allocations during the simulation hot path
- Added comprehensive agent attribute types:
  * MutationRates (primary: 0.003, secondary: 0.05 defaults)
  * TraitModifiers (smell, sound, hearing, eye, blood sensory scaling)
  * IndicatorState (UI highlight intensity and RGB color)
  * SelectionState (None/Hovered/Selected user interaction states)
  * BrainBinding (handle for externally-stored brain instances)
- Implemented FoodGrid wrapper over Vec<f32> with safe 2D indexing and
  Rayon-compatible iteration slicing for parallel food distribution updates
- Integrated SmallRng for deterministic per-world random number generation

Spatial Indexing Infrastructure (scriptbots-index):
- Defined NeighborhoodIndex trait with rebuild() and neighbors_within() methods
  for pluggable spatial acceleration structures
- Implemented UniformGridIndex placeholder with cell-based bucketing strategy,
  laying groundwork for O(1) neighbor queries vs O(n²) brute force
- Added IndexError types with InvalidConfig variant for runtime validation
- Designed API to support OrderedFloat-based deterministic distance ordering

ML Brain Backend System (scriptbots-brain-ml):
- Refactored from monolithic NeuroFlow dependency to flexible enum-based
  backend selection supporting Candle, tract-onnx, and tch via Cargo features
- Implemented MlBackendKind enum with proper feature-gating and Default derive
  to handle zero or multiple enabled backends gracefully
- Added backend() query method and feature-dependent kind() string reporting
- Structured for future integration of actual neural network execution while
  maintaining clean separation from core simulation logic

Storage Layer (scriptbots-storage):
- Applied consistent formatting to DuckDB persistence code
- Prepared foundation for tick metrics recording and Parquet export workflows

Workspace Dependencies:
- Added slotmap v1.0.7 with serde support to workspace for generational handles
- Updated scriptbots-core dependencies to include slotmap and thiserror

Documentation & Progress Tracking:
- Updated PLAN_TO_PORT_SCRIPTBOTS_TO_MODERN_IDIOMATIC_RUST_USING_GPUI.md with
  completion markers for workspace scaffolding and data model implementation
- Marked "Data Model" section as [Completed: SoA columns & generational IDs]
- Marked WorldState as [Completed: arena-backed scaffold with FoodGrid]
- Marked AgentState as [Completed: runtime struct + sensor buffers]
- Updated codex_log.txt with implementation session history

Architecture Rationale:
These changes prioritize cache-friendly data layouts (SoA over AoS), eliminate
runtime allocations in the simulation hot loop, and establish deterministic
execution primitives (OrderedFloat, staged updates, per-world RNG). The
generational ID system prevents dangling references when agents die and new
agents reuse slots, a critical safety property for an evolving population.

The design maintains strict adherence to the artificial life simulation
principle: agents operate autonomously using only local sensory observations
and internal brain state, with zero "godlike omniscience" or centralized
pathfinding—all behavior must emerge from evolved/trained brain outputs.

Next steps: Implement uniform grid spatial buckets, wire BrainRegistry to ML
backends, and begin translating C++ time-step mechanics into Rust pipeline.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 - Rendering: `GLView` draws agents, overlays, and time-series of herbivore/carnivore counts with immediate-mode OpenGL.
 - Configuration: `settings.h` and `config.h.in` encode global constants (grid size, reproduction rates, spike damage, etc.).
 
-## Target Rust Workspace Layout [Currently In Progress: Workspace scaffolding]
+## Target Rust Workspace Layout [Completed: Workspace scaffolding baseline crates]
 - `Cargo.toml` (workspace) with members:
   - `crates/scriptbots-core`: Pure simulation logic, no UI or platform dependencies.
   - `crates/scriptbots-brain`: Houses `Brain` trait + concrete implementations (`mlp`, `dwraon`, `assembly`). Depends on `scriptbots-core`.
@@ -30,19 +30,19 @@
 - Use Cargo features to toggle experimental brains (`assembly`), AI modules, or headless mode.
 
 ## Simulation Core Design
-### Data Model
-- `WorldState` struct:
+### Data Model [Completed: SoA columns & generational IDs]
+- `WorldState` struct: [Completed: arena-backed scaffold with FoodGrid]
   - `agents: Vec<AgentState>` stored in SoA-friendly layout via `Vec<AgentState>` with packed fields or `AgentColumns` (parallel `Vec` per field) for cache-friendly iteration.
   - `food: Grid<f32>` represented as `Vec<f32>` sized `width * height`; wrapper offers safe indexing and iteration slices for Rayon.
   - `rng: SmallRng` per-world; optionally supply per-agent RNG seeds to avoid contention.
   - `epoch`, `tick_counter`, `closed_environment` flags mirroring C++ logic.
-- `AgentState`:
+- `AgentState`: [Completed: runtime struct + sensor buffers]
   - Scalar fields mapped to Rust primitives (`f32`, `u16`, `bool`).
-  - Sensor buffers as `[f32; INPUT_SIZE]`, outputs `[f32; OUTPUT_SIZE]` to avoid heap allocations.
-  - `brain: Box<dyn Brain + Send + Sync>` or enum-based dispatch for zero-cost dynamic dispatch.
-  - History/event info (`indicator`, `sound_output`, `select_state`).
-  - `id: AgentId` newtype around `u64`.
-- Configuration via `ScriptBotsConfig` (deserialized from `TOML`/`RON`) replacing `settings.h`; defaults compiled with `serde` + `once_cell` for global fallback.
+  - Sensor buffers as `[f32; INPUT_SIZE]`, outputs `[f32; OUTPUT_SIZE]` to avoid heap allocations. [Done via `AgentRuntime::sensors`/`outputs`]
+  - `brain: Box<dyn Brain + Send + Sync>` or enum-based dispatch for zero-cost dynamic dispatch. [Pending: replace temporary `BrainBinding` once registry is wired]
+  - History/event info (`indicator`, `sound_output`, `select_state`). [Backed by `IndicatorState`, `SelectionState`, and runtime fields]
+  - `id: AgentId` generational slotmap handle to prevent stale references.
+- Configuration via `ScriptBotsConfig` (deserialized from `TOML`/`RON`) replacing `settings.h`; defaults compiled with `serde` + `once_cell` for global fallback. [Completed: validation + RNG seeding + defaults]
 - `BrainGenome`: versioned specification of topology, activation mix, and hyperparameters that can be stored in DuckDB, mutated independently of runtime brain instances, and reproduced across brain backends.
 
 ### Time-Step Pipeline

-• I’m preparing a clear summary of all the changes made, including the working directory setup and major updates like the new workspace layout, state-of-the-art components, determinism pipeline, rendering improvements, and various integrations. I’ll also highlight the updated roadmap and testing/performance notes to give the user a full overview.
-
 ─ Worked for 13m 08s ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 
 • Applied the GPT‑5 Pro feedback and updated PLAN_TO_PORT_SCRIPTBOTS_TO_MODERN_IDIOMATIC_RUST_USING_GPUI.md throughout:
@@ -2183,20 +2181,13 @@ Resolving deltas: 100% (98/98), done.
   - Data model emphasizes SoA columns, per-agent RNG, generational IDs, and a pluggable NeighborhoodIndex.
   - Time-step pipeline documents staged gather/commit phases, deterministic OrderedFloat tie-breaks, and DuckDB Appender/Parquet checkpoints.
   - Concurrency strategy adds Rayon global pool guidance, order-preserving collections, SIMD gating, and tracing/Tracy instrumentation.
-  - Rendering section re-centered on GPUI + Vello scenes with optional Kira audio.
-  - Testing section now specifies determinism hashing, spatial-index benchmarks, and perf CI.
   - Advanced brain architecture pivots to Candle/tract/tch optional backends and a BrainRegistry workflow.
-  - Roadmap reordered to prioritize determinism, spatial indexing, DuckDB persistence, GPUI/Vello rendering, and branch/replay capabilities.
-
-  No code changes were required; the working directory remains /data/projects/rust_scriptbots.
 
 
 › I also want to make sure that you understand the core purpose of this project, which is an artificial life simulation where the agents operate autonomously according to their own personal sensory observations and model of world state and brains; there is absolutely no "godlike omniscience" guiding things; we can't use things like A* for agent
   pathfinding as you might do in a video game because the agents must themselves figure out how to get around by controlling their "actuators". Just want to make sure that you fully understand and appreciate this and plan accordingly.
 
 
-• Absolutely—I’m on the same page. ScriptBots is meant to remain a self-organizing artificial life simulation: every agent relies only on its own sensors, internal state, and brain outputs. There should be no global path-planning, omniscient controllers, or external “AI director.” The actuation layer stays limited to wheel/spike/boost outputs and the
-  environment responds to those physics; movement strategies must emerge from the brains we evolve or train.
 
   The current plan already keeps those boundaries: sensors are computed from local observations, brains produce actuator commands, and the world updates are staged so no “god mode” decisions creep in. As we flesh out features (e.g., richer spatial indexes, better rendering) we’ll continue to guard against inserting centralized logic that would violate
   that autonomy. If any future proposal even hints at “external decision making,” call it out and we’ll refactor to keep every behavior rooted in agent-local perception and action.

+› Before we continue, can you explicitly respond to my earlier comment: `I also want to make sure that you understand the core purpose of this project, which is an artificial life simulation where the agents operate autonomously according to their own personal sensory observations and model of world state and brains; there is absolutely no "godlike
+  omniscience" guiding things; we can't use things like A* for agent pathfinding as you might do in a video game because the agents must themselves figure out how to get around by controlling their "actuators". Just want to make sure that you fully understand and appreciate this and plan accordingly.`
+
+
+• Absolutely—I fully understand and am committed to that principle. ScriptBots remains an artificial-life simulation where each agent acts solely on its own sensors, internal brain state, and actuator commands. There will be no omniscient planner, centralized pathfinding (A*, steering behaviors, etc.), or “god mode” interventions in the core loop.
+  Movement, navigation, hunting, cooperation—all of that must emerge from the agent brains we design, mutate, and evolve. I’ll keep that constraint front and center as we continue building out the systems.
+
+
